rulings.Case Title: OPTRICS INC ET AL VS. GRACE R. NEIBARON, ESQ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Good Faith Settlement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof.
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 21, 2026, line 1. DEFENDANTS LAW OFFICES OF HERBERT L. TERRERI, APC, HERBERT TERRERI, ESQ.'S Motion For Good Faith Settlement. The motion is granted, and the court finds that the settlement among plaintiff Optrics Inc. and defendants Herbert L. Terreri and Law Offices of Herbert L. Terreri is made in good faith within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure, section 877.6. No party objected to the good-faith finding. Optrics has filed objections to some of the evidence submitted by the Terreri Defendants. Optrics' objections are preserved, but the court finds it unnecessary to rely on the objected-to evidence and therefore does not rule on the objections. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: BRANDON HERRERA ARUIRRE VS. MICHAEL J. TOY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Discovery Response To Demand For Inspection (Set Two), Request For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities In Support Of Motion; Declaration Of Yaelle E. Gurel In Support Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 21, 2026, line 4. DEFENDANT MICHAEL TOY'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Discovery Response To Demand For Inspection (Set Two), Request For Monetary Sanctions. The motion to compel plaintiff's further responses to defendant's second set of inspection demands is denied. The court orders defendant to pay $500 in sanctions to the court within 30 days and to file notice that defendant has complied. The court denies the motion because it is procedurally defective. First, defendant failed to meet and confer. Defendant acknowledges that plaintiff served responses to the discovery; defendant takes issue with the content of the responses and that they included only objections. (Gurel Decl., para. 9.) Defendant therefore seeks further responses and brings this motion under Code of Civil Procedure, section 2031.310, subdivision (a)(3). Such a motion must be accompanied by a meet and confer declaration. (Id, sec. 2031.310, subd. (b)(2).) Defendant provides no evidence of informal resolution efforts. That trial was approaching at the time the document demand was made is a problem of defendant's own making. Second, defendant fails to include either a separate statement or plaintiff's discovery responses. The court cannot rule on the merits of objections that it does not have before it. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2031.310, subd. (b)(3).) Defendant seeks sanctions; plaintiff does not. The court nonetheless orders defendant to pay $500 to the court within 30 days for failing to meet and confer where required. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2031.310, subd. (h); id., sec. 2023.010, subd. (i).) Defendant is further ordered to file a notice with the court once it has complied with the sanction order. If defendant contests the sanctions portion of the tentative ruling, the court will put over the matter for further hearing on the sanction to permit defendant notice. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: MIDLAND CREDIT MANAGEMENT, INC. VS. CHRISTINE CARR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 21, 2026, line 11. DEFENDANT CHRISTINE CARR'S Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents. Defendant Christine Carr's unopposed motion to compel responses to her special interrogatories and requests for production of documents is granted. Plaintiff Midland Credit Management has waived objections by failing to timely respond. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2030.290, subd. (a); id., sec. 2031.300, subd. (a).) Plaintiff is ordered to provide objection-free responses within three weeks. Carr seeks sanctions. The failure to submit to an authorized method of discovery is a misuse of the discovery process and is sanctionable. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2030.290, subd. (c); id., sec. 2031.300, subd. (c); id., sec. 2023.010, subd. (d).) The court does not find substantial justification or unjust circumstances. Plaintiff is ordered to pay Carr $580 within 30 days. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
